title: 46 Greenberg Traurig Attorneys Recognized in 2026 IAM Patent 1000 | News | Greenberg Traurig LLP
content:
NEW YORK – June 5, 2026 – Forty-six attorneys from global law firm Greenberg Traurig, LLP were recognized in the 2026 edition of Intellectual Asset Management (IAM) ’s Patent 1000 – The World’s Leading Patent Professionals . The firm was also recognized nationally in the United States and in nine regions for its patent litigation, prosecution, and transaction capabilities.
The guide identifies the top patent professionals in key jurisdictions around the globe, according to the publication. The patent professionals are identified after a five-month, extensive research process, including approximately 1,800 interviews with attorneys, patent attorneys, and in-house counsel.
The following practitioners were selected:
- Elana B. Araj : Litigation (New York)
- Phillip M. Aurentz : Litigation (Texas)
- Jonathan D. Ball, Ph.D . : Litigation and Prosecution (New York)
- Lennie A. Bersh : Prosecution (New Jersey)
- David S. Bloch : Litigation (California)
- Christopher C. Bolten : Prosecution (California)
- Gregory S. Bombard : Litigation (Massachusetts) and Trade Secret Litigation (National)
- Scott J. Bornstein : Litigation (New York)
- Heath J. Briggs : Prosecution (Colorado) and Post-grant Proceedings (National)
- Nicholas A. Brown : Litigation (California)
- Kyle D. Chen, Ph.D. : Litigation (California)
- James J. DeCarlo : Litigation and Prosecution (New Jersey)

In addition to being recognized nationally in the United States for Prosecution and Trade Secret Litigation, Greenberg Traurig was recommended in the following key areas of practice in nine regions:
- California for Litigation
- Colorado for Prosecution
- Illinois for Prosecution and Litigation
- Massachusetts for Prosecution and Transactions
- Minnesota for Prosecution
- New Jersey for Global Patent Offering
- New York for Prosecution and Litigation
- Texas for Litigation
- Utah for Global Patent Offering
